metadata.title: Guideline on Core SmPC for Human Plasma Derived and Recombinant Coagulation Factor IX Products Rev. 3

rules.rule_text: FIX potency must be determined using the European Pharmacopoeia one stage clotting test. Human coagulation factor IX is defined by Ph. Eur. Monograph 1223.
rules.verbatim_quote: The potency (IU) is determined using the European Pharmacopoeia one stage clotting test.

rules.rule_text: Treatment must be under the supervision of a physician experienced in the treatment of haemophilia. Precise monitoring by coagulation analysis is indispensable for major surgical interventions.
rules.verbatim_quote: Treatment should be under the supervision of a physician experienced in the treatment of haemophilia. ... In the case of major surgical interventions in particular, precise monitoring of the substitution therapy by means of coagulation analysis (plasma factor IX activity) is indispensable.

rules.rule_text: FIX dosing levels for on-demand treatment must be specified: early haemarthrosis/muscle/oral (20-40 IU/dl), extensive haemarthrosis/haematoma (30-60 IU/dl), life-threatening (60-100 IU/dl), minor surgery (30-60 IU/dl), major surgery (80-100 IU/dl pre- and post-operative).
rules.verbatim_quote: In the case of the following haemorrhagic events, the factor IX activity should not fall below the given plasma activity level

rules.rule_text: For long-term prophylaxis in severe haemophilia B, the usual doses are 20-40 IU/kg body weight at intervals of 3-4 days.
rules.verbatim_quote: For long term prophylaxis against bleeding in patients with severe haemophilia B, the usual doses are 20 to 40 IU of factor IX per kilogram of body weight at intervals of 3 to 4 days.
rules.exceptions: In some cases, especially in younger patients, shorter dosage intervals or higher doses may be necessary.

rules.rule_text: The SmPC must include a warning about the correlation between FIX inhibitor occurrence and allergic/anaphylactic reactions. Patients experiencing allergic reactions must be evaluated for FIX inhibitors.
rules.verbatim_quote: There have been reports in the literature showing a correlation between the occurrence of a factor IX inhibitor and allergic reactions. Therefore, patients experiencing allergic reactions should be evaluated for the presence of an inhibitor. It should be noted that patients with factor IX inhibitors may be at an increased risk of anaphylaxis with subsequent challenge with factor IX.

rules.rule_text: Initial administrations of FIX must be performed under medical observation where proper medical care for allergic reactions could be provided, according to the treating physician's judgement.
rules.verbatim_quote: Because of the risk of allergic reactions with factor IX products, the initial administrations of factor IX should, according to the treating physician's judgement, be performed under medical observation where proper medical care for allergic reactions could be provided.

rules.rule_text: The SmPC must include a thromboembolism warning: clinical surveillance for early signs of thrombotic and consumptive coagulopathy must be initiated with appropriate biological testing in patients with pre-existing risk factors, liver disease, post-operatively, in new-born infants, or patients at risk of DIC.
rules.verbatim_quote: Because of the potential risk of thrombotic complications, clinical surveillance for early signs of thrombotic and consumptive coagulopathy should be initiated with appropriate biological testing when administering this medicinal product to patients with pre-existing risk factors, e.g. liver disease, to patients post-operatively, to new-born infants, or to patients at risk of thrombotic phenomena or DIC.

rules.rule_text: The SmPC must warn that in patients with existing cardiovascular risk factors, substitution therapy with FIX may further increase cardiovascular risk.
rules.verbatim_quote: In patients with existing cardiovascular risk factors, substitution therapy with factor IX may further increase the cardiovascular risk.

rules.rule_text: The SmPC undesirable effects section must state that nephrotic syndrome has been reported following attempted immune tolerance induction in haemophilia B patients with FIX inhibitors and a history of allergic reaction.
rules.verbatim_quote: Nephrotic syndrome has been reported following attempted immune tolerance induction in haemophilia B patients with factor IX inhibitors and a history of allergic reaction.

rules.rule_text: The SmPC must warn of the potential risk of thromboembolic episodes following FIX administration, with higher risk for low purity preparations. Low purity products have been associated with myocardial infarction, DIC, venous thrombosis, and pulmonary embolism.
rules.verbatim_quote: There is a potential risk of thromboembolic episodes following the administration of factor IX products, with a higher risk for low purity preparations. The use of low purity factor IX products has been associated with instances of myocardial infarction, disseminated intravascular coagulation, venous thrombosis and pulmonary embolism.
rules.exceptions: The use of high purity factor IX is rarely associated with such adverse reactions.

rules.rule_text: The SmPC must note that annualized bleeding rate (ABR) is not comparable between different factor concentrates and between different clinical studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Of note, ABR (annualized bleeding rate) is not comparable between different factor concentrates and between different clinical studies.

rules.rule_text: The SmPC PK section must include data on incremental recovery, AUC, half-life (initial phase and elimination), and clearance.
rules.verbatim_quote: Description of: incremental recovery, area under the curve (AUC), half-life (both the initial phase and elimination half-life), clearance

rules.rule_text: Traceability must be maintained by clearly recording the name and batch number of the administered product.
rules.verbatim_quote: In order to improve traceability of biological medicinal products, the name and the batch number of the administered product should be clearly recorded.

rules.rule_text: aPTT-based assay discrepancies with FIX products must be disclosed in the SmPC when applicable. FIX activity results can be significantly affected by both the aPTT reagent type and reference standard.
rules.verbatim_quote: When using an in vitro thromboplastin time (aPTT)-based one stage clotting assay for determining factor IX activity in patients' blood samples, plasma factor IX activity results can be significantly affected by both the type of aPTT reagent and the reference standard used in the assay.
rules.exceptions: Only where there can be discrepant assay results.

rules.rule_text: FIX should be used during pregnancy and lactation only if clearly indicated, as animal reproduction studies have not been conducted.
rules.verbatim_quote: Animal reproduction studies have not been conducted with factor IX. ... Therefore, factor IX should be used during pregnancy and lactation only if clearly indicated.

rules.rule_text: The medicinal product must not be mixed with other medicinal products. Only provided injection/infusion sets should be used due to risk of FIX adsorption to some equipment surfaces.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the absence of compatibility studies, this medicinal product must not be mixed with other medicinal products. ... Only the provided <injection> <infusion> sets should be used because treatment failure can occur as a consequence of human coagulation factor IX adsorption to the internal surfaces of some <injection> <infusion> equipment.
rules.exceptions: If an injection/infusion set is not provided, information should be included on suitable sets.
